page: Microsoft Azure D11_v2 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. The instance provides 2 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory, yielding 7.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 50 MB of L3 cache. Additionally, the instance includes 102 GB of bundled local storage, but does not feature any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the D11_v2 sits in the lower tiers of performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1118.09 is categorized as weak, while the multi-core score of 2171.76 falls into the poor category. Memory bandwidth tests show poor results, with read-only small block cached speeds at 144.09 GB/sec and large block uncached write speeds at 11.42 GB/sec. Geekbench scores further reflect this trend, with a single-core score of 800.0 and a multi-core score of 1425.0. Database operations via Passmark scored 1108.39, and Redis SET operations achieved 2.22 million operations per second, both indicating limited throughput for data-intensive tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D11_v2 offers a moderate amount of memory relative to its vCPU count, but its older Intel Xeon architecture results in low benchmark-per-resource tradeoffs compared to newer hardware generations. The bundled 102 GB of local storage provides some value for workloads requiring temporary disk space. Given the weak CPU and memory performance metrics, this instance is recommended for light general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, small-scale database operations, or development and testing environments that do not require high computational throughput or advanced vector extensions.
description: Microsoft Azure D11_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is configured with 14.0 GB of RAM, 102 GB of bundled local storage, and no GPUs. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance exhibits lower-tier performance across benchmarks. Its stress-ng single-core score of 1118.09 is weak, and its multi-core score of 2171.76 is poor. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large-block write speeds at 11.42 GB/sec. Given these performance constraints, the D11_v2 is recommended for low-intensity workloads, such as basic web serving, lightweight database operations, or development and testing tasks that benefit from a higher memory-to-core ratio without requiring high computational throughput.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D11_v2 is a Dv2-family virtual machine with 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 14 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D11_v2 is a Dv2 virtual machine featuring 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 14 GB of RAM, and 102 GB of local storage for basic workloads.
tagline: An entry-level virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, balanced memory allocation, and bundled local storage for basic computing tasks.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 physical cores at 2.3 GHz
Equipped with 14.0 GB of system memory and 102 GB of bundled local storage
Delivers 2.22 million Redis SET operations per second
Features 50 MB of L3 cache and support for AVX and AES instructions
Recommended for light web serving, development environments, and basic database tasks
